info.name: Cardioplace v2 — Alert Engine Tests
info.description:
Postman collection mirroring docs/POSTMAN_ALERT_TESTING.md.

Import this + the cardioplace-dev environment file.

Workflow per scenario:
1. Run a request from '1. Setup' to populate {{accessToken}} or {{mdAccessToken}}.
2. Run a scenario request from '3. Category A' or '4. Category B'.
3. Run 'List my alerts' from '2. Helpers' to see what fired.

Collection-level pre-request script auto-sets {{measuredAt}} to now-ISO before every request, so payloads always pass the 30-day-window validator.

exec: // Refresh measuredAt for every request so the backend's 30-day window validator passes.

item.name: 1. Setup — Authentication
item.description: Run these in order. Patient OTP → token, then MD OTP → mdToken (Category B only).

item.name: Send OTP — Medical Director (Category B prerequisite)
2. Helpers — Verify state
item.description: Run these any time to inspect what the engine has done.

item.name: Get latest alert by entryId (after a scenario)

exec: // Endpoint returns { statusCode, message, data: DeviationAlert[] } — unwrap.
const matching = alerts.filter(a => a.journalEntry && a.journalEntry.id === entryId);
console.log('Found ' + matching.length + ' alert(s) for entryId ' + entryId + ':');
matching.forEach(a => console.log('  -', a.ruleId, '/', a.tier, '/ dismissible=' + a.dismissible));
// Endpoint returns { statusCode, message, data: PatientMedication[] } — unwrap.
console.warn('No medications on file — Scenario 61 (per-med miss) needs at least one med.');

item.name: 3. Category A — No threshold needed
item.description: Standard-mode scenarios. No PatientThreshold required. Each request POSTs a journal entry and saves entryId for follow-up alert verification via the helper.

exec: console.log('Now run: 2. Helpers → Get latest alert by entryId');

item.name: Scenario 51 — Pregnancy + ACE + 195/130 (Tier 1 beats BP L2)

exec: console.log('Expected: RULE_ABSOLUTE_EMERGENCY / BP_LEVEL_2 / pulsePressure=85 / patientMessage contains 911');

item.name: A3. Symptom overrides (BP L2 at any BP)
Scenario 22 — Pregnant + ruqPain (login as pregnant user without ACE/ARB)
Scenario 7 — Pregnancy L1 High 144/88 (clean pregnant patient)
Scenario 9 (1/3) — AFib + 3 readings, pulse 115 (charles.brown — call THREE times)

exec: console.log('Run this request THREE times within 30 minutes (same sessionUuid). Expected on 3rd: RULE_AFIB_HR_HIGH');
console.log('Expected: RULE_STANDARD_L1_HIGH + pulsePressure=84 + physicianMessage with PP annotation');
console.log('Expected: RULE_STANDARD_L1_HIGH + suboptimalMeasurement=true + retake in patientMessage');

body.raw:
{
  "measuredAt": "{{measuredAt}}",
  "systolicBP": 164,
  "diastolicBP": 96,
  "pulse": 78,
  "position": "SITTING",
  "measurementConditions": {
    "noCaffeine": false,
    "noSmoking": true,
    "noExercise": true,
    "bladderEmpty": true,
    "seatedQuietly": true,
    "posturalSupport": true,
    "notTalking": true,
    "cuffOnBareArm": true
  }
}

exec: console.log('Expected: RULE_CAD_DBP_CRITICAL / type=DIASTOLIC_BP / patientMessage contains lower number');
console.log('Expected: RULE_AGE_65_LOW / patientMessage contains dizziness + fall risk');

item.name: Scenario 36 — Brady asymptomatic pulse 38 (custom hasBradycardia, no BB)
Scenario 61 — Per-medication miss (Lisinopril FORGOT — fill medicationId from helper first)

exec: console.log('Expected: physicianMessage contains Lisinopril + ACE_INHIBITOR + FORGOT + doses missed: 1');

item.name: Scenario 62 — Co-occurrence: BP L1 High + missed med (TWO alert rows)

exec: console.log('Expected: TWO rows. Row 1: RULE_STANDARD_L1_HIGH (type=SYSTOLIC_BP). Row 2: RULE_MEDICATION_MISSED (type=MEDICATION_ADHERENCE).');

item.name: Scenario 63 — Happy path (no alert, resolve-sweep only)

exec: console.log('Expected: NO new alert row. updateMany scoped to BP_LEVEL_1_HIGH/LOW only — Tier 2 + Tier 1 + BP L2 preserved.');
console.log('Expected: NO new alert. Any prior OPEN BP_LEVEL_1_* rows now status=RESOLVED.');

item.name: Scenario 17 — AFib + 1 reading + pulse 118 (gate closed, charles)

exec: console.log('Expected: NO row (AFib gate requires 3+ readings)');

item.name: Scenario 19 — Brady + BB pulse 55 (suppression window, custom)
4. Category B — Threshold required (PERSONALIZED)
item.description: Only TWO rules need a provider-set threshold + ≥7 prior readings.

item.name: B1. Set threshold (uses {{mdAccessToken}} — sign in as MD first)

exec: console.log('Threshold set. Now log 7+ benign readings, then run B2 or B3.');

body.raw:
{
  "sbpUpperTarget": 130,
  "sbpLowerTarget": 90,
  "dbpUpperTarget": null,
  "dbpLowerTarget": null,
  "hrUpperTarget": null,
  "hrLowerTarget": null,
  "notes": "Postman test — set via Cardioplace Alert collection"
}

item.name: B-Prep. Log a benign reading (run 7× before B2/B3 to satisfy ≥7 readings)

exec: console.log('Benign reading #' + n + ' / 7 logged for Personalized rule prerequisite');

item.name: B2. Personalized HIGH — SBP 152 (threshold upper=130 + ≥7 readings)
B3. Personalized LOW — SBP 108 (threshold lower=110 + ≥7 readings — re-run B1 with sbpLowerTarget:110 first)
B4. Edge — threshold present but <7 readings (pre-Day-3 disclaimer)

exec: console.log('Expected: RULE_STANDARD_L1_HIGH (NOT _PERSONALIZED_) + mode=STANDARD + patientMessage matches /personalization begins after Day 3/i');

item.name: B5. Edge — threshold required but NOT set + payload sent (graceful no-op)

exec: console.log('Expected: NO row. SBP 152 < 160 standard cutoff, no threshold to compare against. Engine never errors.');
console.log('Sign in as a brand-new patient who has NOT completed POST /api/intake/profile to see this.');

item.name: Bad payload — SBP out of range → 400
7. Alert resolution (admin) — §12
item.description: Workflow: 1) Trigger an alert (Category A). 2) Run 'Save first OPEN alertId' to capture it. 3) Sign in as MD or Provider. 4) Run acknowledge / resolve / audit requests. Maps to E2E_TEST_CASES.md §12 (TC-RES.*).

exec: // Endpoint returns { statusCode, message, data: DeviationAlert[] } — unwrap.
console.warn('No OPEN alert found — trigger a Category A scenario first.');

item.name: TC-RES.20 — Resolve Tier 1 with NO rationale → 400
TC-RES.23 — Wrong action enum (BP_L2 on Tier 1) → 400

exec: console.log('Expected: 400 "Alert is already resolved". Run TC-RES.10 first to resolve, then this.');

item.name: TC-RES.30 — Resolve Tier 2: REVIEWED_NO_ACTION without rationale → 400

exec: console.log('REVIEWED_NO_ACTION is the only Tier 2 action that requires rationale.');

item.name: TC-RES.32 — Resolve Tier 2: WILL_CONTACT (no rationale needed)
TC-RES.33 — Resolve Tier 2: CHANGE_ORDERED (no rationale needed)
TC-RES.34 — Resolve Tier 2: PHARMACY_RECONCILE (no rationale needed)
TC-RES.35 — Resolve Tier 2: DEFERRED (no rationale needed)
TC-RES.40 — Resolve BP L2: CONTACTED_MED_ADJUSTED + rationale
TC-RES.41 — Resolve BP L2: CONTACTED_ADVISED_ED + rationale
TC-RES.42 — Resolve BP L2: CONTACTED_RECHECK + rationale
TC-RES.43 — Resolve BP L2: SEEN_IN_OFFICE + rationale
TC-RES.44 — Resolve BP L2: REVIEWED_TRENDING_DOWN + rationale
TC-RES.45 — Resolve BP L2: UNABLE_TO_REACH_RETRY (alert stays OPEN, retry scheduled)

exec: console.log('Expected: status=OPEN; fresh EscalationEvent with triggeredByResolution=true; scheduledFor +4h');

item.name: TC-RES.46 — Resolve BP L2 with no rationale → 400
8. Patient escalations + notifications — §11.2
item.description: Patient-side reads for escalation events and push notifications. Maps to TC-RDG.10–15.

exec: console.log('Returns escalation events for THIS patient. Empty array if no alerts have escalated yet.');
// Endpoint returns { statusCode, message, data: Notification[] } — unwrap.
console.warn('No notifications yet. Trigger an alert first to populate.');

item.name: TC-RDG.14 — Mark a notification as watched
TC-RDG.15 — Bulk mark notifications as watched
9. RBAC — alert endpoints — §15
item.description: Negative auth tests for alert resolution endpoints. Maps to TC-RBAC.17, TC-RBAC.18, TC-RBAC.24, TC-RBAC.44.

item.name: Send OTP — HEALPLACE_OPS (helper, run before TC-RBAC.44)
10. Escalation ladder — §13 (notes only)
item.description:
Escalation ladder testing requires time-travel via EscalationService.runScan(now) — a Nest service method, NOT an HTTP endpoint. Postman cannot test §13 directly. Use Jest integration tests in backend/test/escalation.e2e-spec.ts instead.

What you CAN do via Postman:
• Trigger an alert (Category A) → query GET /api/daily-journal/escalations as that patient → see EscalationEvent rows the engine has already persisted.
• Wait 15 minutes for the real cron to fire (@Cron('*/15 * * * *')) → re-query.
• For deterministic tests, run: cd backend && npx jest escalation.e2e-spec

This folder contains only pointer requests for visibility.

item.name: GET escalation events for patient (after triggering an alert)
